title: hieracium murorum subsp. monochroum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. monochroum, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its yellow flowers and bitter taste. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, due to its anthraquinone content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in soothing skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Hieracium murorum subsp. monochroum is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or dried extracts, although its use should be cautioned against due to potential side effects.
